status.launchingHint: When prompted by your browser, click "Open" to launch the app
status.failedHint: Helper may not be installed. Please download it from the link below.

forgotPassword.description: Enter your email address. We will send you a password reset link.
forgotPassword.emailSentTo: A reset link has been sent to:
forgotPassword.instruction1: Click the link in the email to reset your password.
forgotPassword.requestSuccessDesc: A password reset link has been sent by email
forgotPassword.title: Password Reset

auth.forgotPasswordDesc: We'll send a reset link to your registered email

resetPassword.invalidToken: Invalid reset link. Please access again from the link in your email.
resetPassword.completeDesc: Your password has been changed successfully
resetPassword.loginPrompt: Please sign in with your new password.

auth.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 8 characters
auth.passwordRequirements: Password must be at least 8 characters with uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and special characters
auth.resetEmailSent: If the email address is registered, a password reset email has been sent. Please check your email.
auth.enterRegisteredEmail: Please enter your registered email address. We will send you a password reset link.

passkey.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this passkey?
passkey.loginError: Failed to sign in with passkey
passkey.notSupported: Your browser does not support passkey authentication
passkey.registerDesc: Register a passkey on this device to log in without a password next time.
passkey.manageDesc: Manage your registered passkeys. You can delete unnecessary passkeys.
passkey.deleteConfirmDesc: If you delete this passkey, you will not be able to log in with passkey on this device.
passkey.recoveryCodeDesc: Emergency codes that can be used if you lose access to your passkeys. Each code can only be used once.
passkey.regenerateCodesConfirm: All existing recovery codes will be invalidated. Generate new codes?
passkey.generateCodesRequirement: ※Register at least one passkey before generating
passkey.passkeyOnlyModeDesc: Disable password login and sign in with passkeys only. This significantly improves security.
passkey.passkeyOnlyModeRequirement: * Register at least one passkey before enabling
passkey.recoveryNote: * If you lose your passkey, you can recover with recovery codes or by email

verification.title: Email Verification
verification.emailSentTo: A verification email has been sent to:
verification.instruction1: Click the link in the email to activate your account.
verification.instruction2: If you don't see the email, check your spam folder.
verification.errorInstruction: The link may be invalid or expired.

recovery.requestDesc: If you can't access your passkey, you can recover your account with your email.
recovery.requestSuccessDesc: A recovery link has been sent to your email.
recovery.emailSentTo: A recovery link has been sent to:
recovery.instruction1: Click the link in the email to recover your account.
recovery.successDesc: Your account has been recovered and you are now logged in.
recovery.redirecting: Redirecting to home page in {count} seconds...
recovery.registerPasskeyRecommendation: For security, we recommend registering a new passkey.
recovery.errorInstruction: The link may be invalid or expired.

2fa.title: Two-Factor Authentication
2fa.expiresIn: The code is valid for 10 minutes
2fa.settingsDescription: When logging in with a password, you'll also need to enter a 6-digit code sent to your email.
2fa.howItWorks: When enabled, a 6-digit verification code will be sent to your registered email address during password login. Enter this code to complete the login.
2fa.passkeyNote: * Two-factor authentication is not required for passkey or recovery code login.
2fa.enableConfirm: You will need to enter a verification code sent by email when logging in with a password. Continue?
2fa.disableConfirm: This will lower your security level. Enter your password to disable.

auth.forgotPasswordHint: Send a reset link to your registered email address
auth.passkeyOnlyModeNotice: This account can only be accessed with a passkey.
auth.passkeyOnlyRecoveryHint: If you cannot use your passkey, please use the "Recovery" tab.
auth.recoveryCodeHint: Sign in using a recovery code you saved earlier
auth.recoveryEmailHint: Enter the email address of the account to recover

recoveryCode.title: Sign in with Recovery Code
recoveryCode.description1: If you lose your passkey, you can sign in using a recovery code.
recoveryCode.description2: Each code can be used only once.
recoveryCode.registerNewPasskeyWarning: ⚠️ Please register a new passkey after signing in

recoveryCodesDisplay.title: Please Save Your Recovery Codes
recoveryCodesDisplay.description1: If you lose your passkey, you can recover your account with these codes.
recoveryCodesDisplay.description2: Each code can be used only once. Store them in a safe place.
recoveryCodesDisplay.warning: ⚠️ Important: These codes will never be shown again. Make sure to save them.

home.title: DigiCode
home.subtitle: Visual Programming Environment for ESP32
home.heroText2: Easily control robots and IoT devices by combining blocks.
home.firmwareWriteDesc: Write the latest firmware to ESP32
home.pinSettingsDesc: Configure pin numbers for robot and sensors
home.supportedDevicesDesc: Compatible with ESP32 series and various sensors and motors
home.esp32OnlyWarning: This editor is for ESP32 only. robots using Arduino Nano cannot be used as-is. You need to replace it with ESP32.

projects.title: Project List
projects.deleteConfirmMessage: This action cannot be undone. Are you sure you want to delete?

editor.title: Block Editor
editor.unsavedChanges: You have unsaved changes. Discard and create new?
editor.arduinoOnly: Compile is only available for Arduino C++ language.
editor.deviceNotFound: Device "{{hostname}}" not found. Check power and WiFi connection.
editor.deviceNotResponding: Device not responding. Check power and WiFi connection.
editor.compileTicketSaved: Compile was not executed to save compile tickets.
editor.selectAnotherMethod: Select another write method or check device power and WiFi connection.
editor.connectionToEsp32Failed:
Failed to connect to ESP32.
Check if device is connected.
editor.writingComplete:
Firmware write complete!
ESP32 will restart.
editor.otaComplete:
OTA update complete!
ESP32 will restart.
editor.compilingForEsp32: Compiling Arduino C++ code for ESP32

menu.noLimitLocal: No limit when using local server

flash.title: Compile & Write
flash.selectMethod: Select write method to compile and write.
flash.doNotClose: Do not close this window while writing

codePreview.title: Generated Code ({{language}})
codePreview.description: Arduino C++ code generated from Blockly blocks

deviceSelector.noOnlineDevices: No online devices found. Please check if the device is powered on and the IP address is correct.
deviceSelector.searchError: An error occurred while searching for devices
deviceSelector.noIpAddress:
This device has no IP address set.
Please reconfigure from "Connection" → "WiFi Setup".
deviceSelector.offlineWarning:
The device may be offline.
• Check if the device is powered on
• Check if connected to the same WiFi network
• If in a different location, reconfigure from "Connection" → "WiFi Setup"

Select anyway?
deviceSelector.selectionError: An error occurred while selecting the device

wifiDeviceSelect.title: Select WiFi Device
wifiDeviceSelect.description: Select the target device for writing
wifiDeviceSelect.helperDescription: Select a device detected by Helper
wifiDeviceSelect.searchHint: Make sure the device is powered on, then search
wifiDeviceSelect.clipboardDescription: Paste device info copied from DigiCode Finder
wifiDeviceSelect.clipboardHint: Click "Copy device info" in DigiCode Finder

limitReached.title: Compilation Limit Reached
limitReached.description: You have reached this month's cloud compilation limit. Please choose one of the options below.
limitReached.option1Title: Option 1: Use the local compilation environment
limitReached.option1Desc: By running the compilation server locally with Docker, you can compile without limits for free.
limitReached.option2Desc: You can switch to a plan that allows more compilations.

adc2Warning.title: ADC2 Pin Usage Warning
adc2Warning.description: OTA firmware uses WiFi continuously, so analog reads on ADC2 pins will not work correctly.
adc2Warning.pinWarning: Using ADC2 pin for analog input
adc2Warning.recommendation: Recommended: Switch to ADC1 pins (GPIO 32, 33, 34, 35, 36, 39). Digital I/O is not affected.

project.title: Projects
project.deleteConfirm: Delete this project? This action cannot be undone.
project.titleMaxLength: Title must be 100 characters or less
project.selectCategorySample: Select a category to load samples
project.sampleLoadNote: Selecting a sample will load it into the current workspace

device.title: Device Settings
device.wifiRouterConnectDesc: Configure WiFi connection settings for home router or tethering on ESP32
device.webSerialNotSupported: Web Serial API is not supported in this browser. Please use Chrome or Edge browser.
device.selectOrEnterWifi: Select a WiFi or enter new WiFi information.
device.wifiMaxReached: Maximum 5 WiFi networks can be saved. Delete an existing WiFi to add a new one.
device.deviceNameDesc: A name to identify your device. Useful when you have multiple devices on the same network.

deviceNameError.invalidChars: Only alphanumeric characters and hyphens are allowed

device.savedAndRestarted: Settings saved and ESP32 restarted. Connecting to WiFi...
device.selectBatchDevicesDesc: Select multiple devices to write firmware
device.selectNetworkDeviceDesc: Select a DigiCode device on your network
device.checkDeviceConnection: Check if the device is powered on and connected to the same network.
device.deviceNotRegistered: Device not registered. Please set up the device via "Connect to WiFi Router" in the connection menu.
device.usbRequired: USB connection required. Please connect via the "USB (Wired)" tab.
device.uuidAutoGenerated: UUID is auto-generated. Just enter the device name.

batchUpdate.warningDoNotPowerOff: Do not turn off devices during the update

nameDialog.title: Set Device Name
nameDialog.description: Configure the ESP32 device name. Used for identification via BLE/WiFi OTA.
nameDialog.waitingHint: Please wait while boot completion is detected
nameDialog.macWarningBody: macOS aggressively caches BLE device names. Even after changing the name, BLE scans may show the old name (UUID-based) for some time. Windows reflects changes correctly. This is a macOS limitation — the device itself saves the new name properly.

validation.invalidChar: Only alphanumeric characters and hyphens (-) are allowed
validation.hyphenPosition: Hyphens cannot be at the start or end

status.applyingReset: Restarting to apply the new name...
status.saveCompleteResetTimeout: Save complete. Restart timed out (name was saved).

bluetooth.notSupported: Web Bluetooth API is not supported
bluetooth.notSupportedDesc: Bluetooth connection is not available in this browser. Please use the latest version of Chrome or Edge.
bluetooth.step1: Enable BLE UART service on ESP32
bluetooth.step4: Select ESP32 from the device list
bluetooth.hintDesc: Nordic UART Service (NUS) must be implemented on the ESP32 side.

settings.title: Settings
settings.compileServerDesc: Select the server to use for program compilation
settings.cloudDesc: Fast compilation via internet. Always uses the latest libraries.
settings.localDesc: Run compile server locally with Docker. Works offline.
settings.localNotRunning: Local server is not running. Please start it with Docker.
settings.localNoCount: Using local server does not count toward compile limits.
settings.loginRequiredForCloud: Login required to use cloud compilation. Local compile server remains available.
settings.limitReachedMsg: Compile limit reached. Please use local server or consider upgrading your plan.

sidebar.requiresPro: Available with Pro plan or higher
sidebar.hint: Click left arrow to collapse sidebar

wifiSetup.connectingSuccess: WiFi connection successful. Pinning the DHCP-acquired IP as static...
wifiSetup.waitingSystemReady: Please wait until "System Ready!" is detected
wifiSetup.staticIpAutoHint: 💡 Reset on restart; the IP newly acquired via DHCP will be pinned automatically

pinPreset.title: Pin Preset Management
pinPreset.description: Select the default pin number preset, or create your own custom preset

sections.servoPulseDesc: Select the type of servo motor you are using. The pulse width is set automatically, but can be fine-tuned as needed.

favoriteSettings.title: ⭐ Favorite Block Settings
favoriteSettings.description: Select the block categories you use frequently. Selected categories will appear in "Favorites" mode.

usbPortRelease.title: Release USB Ports
usbPortRelease.warning: Use this on Windows when ports are not released and you cannot connect. The page will reload after execution.
usbPortRelease.successAlert:
✓ All ports released.
Please reload the page.

usbSetup.title: USB Wired Connection
usbSetup.description: Connect to the ESP32 via a USB cable

step1.title: USB Connection
step1.description: Connect the ESP32 to your PC with a USB cable

usbSetup.resetDesc: Restart the device. Use this to verify program behavior.
usbSetup.portNotReleasedDesc: On Windows, if ports cannot be released and you cannot connect, use this button to force-release.

usbDriver.title: USB Drivers
usbDriver.description: If your device is not recognized, install the USB serial driver. Download the driver that matches the chip on your ESP32 board.
usbDriver.linuxBuiltIn: Included in the kernel (usually no installation needed)
usbDriver.tip: 💡 Tip: If you don't know which chip your board has, check the underside of the board or its product page. It's often printed near the USB connector.

cp210x.description: USB-UART bridge chip used in many ESP32 development boards

ch340.description: USB-UART bridge chip commonly used in budget ESP32 development boards

ftdi.description: USB-UART bridge chip used in high-quality ESP32 development boards

wifiPrerequisites.title: Required Software
wifiPrerequisites.description: The following software is required to write programs via WiFi OTA.
wifiPrerequisites.finderDesc: A desktop app that automatically discovers DigiCode devices on the network. Used to obtain the device's IP address during WiFi OTA writing.
wifiPrerequisites.bonjourDesc: The mDNS service required for DigiCode Finder to discover devices. Not needed on macOS/Linux (pre-installed).
wifiPrerequisites.restartRequired: ⚠ A PC restart is required after installation.

mobileWarning.title: Please Use the Desktop Version
mobileWarning.description: DigiCode is a PC-only block editor. All features—firmware flashing, device settings, and more—are designed for desktop use.
mobileWarning.notSupported: Smartphones and tablets are not supported.

i2c.scanTooltip: Scan for connected I2C devices and print addresses to Serial
i2c.writeTooltip: Write a byte to an I2C device
i2c.readTooltip: Read a byte from an I2C device
i2c.writeRegTooltip: Write a value to a specific register of an I2C device
i2c.readRegTooltip: Read a value from a specific register of an I2C device

spi.beginTooltip: Initialize SPI communication with chip select pin
spi.transferTooltip: Send a byte via SPI and receive a byte back

interrupt.attachTooltip: Attach an interrupt handler to a pin. Handler runs when the pin state changes. Avoid delay() and Serial inside the handler.
interrupt.detachTooltip: Remove the interrupt handler from a pin
interrupt.checkTooltip: Check and execute the interrupt handler if triggered. Place this in the loop block.

cam.initTooltip: Initialize camera. Select board model — pins are auto-configured. Returns true if successful. Call in setup block.
cam.captureTooltip: Take a photo and store in internal buffer. Returns true if successful. Call camera_save_sd or camera_send_http next.
cam.saveSdTooltip: Save captured image to SD card as JPEG. Call camera_capture first. Returns true if saved. Requires SD initialized (sd_begin).
cam.sendHttpTooltip: Send captured image via HTTP POST. Call camera_capture first. WiFi required. Returns true if sent.
cam.streamTooltip: Start MJPEG streaming server on specified port. WiFi required. View in browser: http://<ESP32-IP>:<port>/stream

can.initTooltip: Initialize CAN bus with TX/RX pins and baud rate. A CAN transceiver IC (e.g. SN65HVD230) is required between ESP32 and CAN bus. Returns true if successful.
can.sendTooltip: Send a CAN message with specified ID and data string (max 8 bytes). ID is an integer (e.g. 0x123 = 291).
can.availableTooltip: Returns true if a new CAN message was received. After true, use can_get_id and can_get_data. Place in loop block.
can.getIdTooltip: Get the ID of the last received CAN message. Call after can_receive_available returns true.
can.getDataTooltip: Get the data of the last received CAN message as a String. Call after can_receive_available returns true.

ir.receiverInitTooltip: Initialize IR receiver on the specified pin. Call in setup block.
ir.receiverDecodeTooltip: Read received IR code as hex string. Returns "0" if no signal. Place in loop block and compare to known codes.
ir.senderInitTooltip: Initialize IR sender LED on the specified pin. Call in setup block.
ir.sendTooltip: Send an IR signal with the specified protocol and code. Use code captured by ir_receiver_decode.

tft.initTooltip: Initialize TFT display. Select driver (ILI9341/ST7789/ST7735) and set CS/DC/RST pins. Uses hardware SPI.
tft.fillScreenTooltip: Fill the entire screen with one color.
tft.pixelTooltip: Draw a single pixel at (X, Y).
tft.rectTooltip: Draw a rectangle. Uncheck fill for outline only, check fill for solid rectangle.
tft.circleTooltip: Draw a circle centered at (X,Y) with radius R. Check fill for solid circle.
tft.setCursorTooltip: Set text cursor position, size and color. Call before tft_print. Size 1=6x8px, 2=12x16px etc.
tft.printTooltip: Print text at the current cursor position. Call tft_set_cursor first.
tft.colorRgbTooltip: Convert RGB values (0-255 each) to 16-bit RGB565 color code for TFT drawing blocks.

rfid.initM5Tooltip: Initialize M5Stack RFID 2 Unit (WS1850S) via I2C. Address 0x28, no CS/RST needed. This device is certified for use in Japan (技適).
rfid.initGenericTooltip:
⚠️ Initialize generic MFRC522 via SPI.

IMPORTANT: In Japan, devices transmitting at 13.56 MHz require 技適 (radio law) certification. Using non-certified MFRC522 modules in Japan may violate the Radio Act. Use rfid_init_m5stack for Japan.

For use outside Japan: check your local radio regulations for 13.56 MHz RFID devices.
rfid.isPresentTooltip: Returns true if an RFID card/tag is detected. Place in loop block.
rfid.readUidTooltip: Read the card UID as hex string (e.g. "04:A3:B5:C9"). Call after rfid_is_card_present returns true.
rfid.readDataTooltip: Read 16 bytes from the specified block (0-63). Uses default auth key (0xFF×6). Avoid block 0.
rfid.writeDataTooltip: Write up to 16 chars to specified block. Returns true on success. Avoid block 0 and sector trailers (3,7,11...).

ws.connectTooltip: Connect to a WebSocket server. Returns true if successful. Requires WiFi connection.
ws.sendTooltip: Send a text message to the connected WebSocket server.
ws.onMessageTooltip: Execute handler when a WebSocket message is received. Use wsMessage variable. Place in loop block.
ws.isConnectedTooltip: Returns true if currently connected to a WebSocket server.

uart2.beginTooltip: Initialize Serial2 (UART2) with specified baud rate and GPIO pins. Default: RX=16, TX=17.
uart2.printTooltip: Send data via Serial2 without newline.
uart2.printlnTooltip: Send data via Serial2 with newline at the end.
uart2.readTooltip: Read one byte from Serial2. Returns -1 if no data available.
uart2.readUntilTooltip: Read from Serial2 until the terminator character. Returns the received string.
uart2.availableTooltip: Returns the number of bytes available to read from Serial2.

dfplayer.initTooltip: Initialize DFPlayer Mini via SoftwareSerial. Connect DFPlayer TX to ESP32 RX, and DFPlayer RX (via 1kΩ resistor) to ESP32 TX.
dfplayer.playTooltip: Play the specified track number (1-999) from the SD card.

lcd.initTooltip: Initialize LCD via I2C. Common addresses: 0x27 (PCF8574) or 0x3F (PCF8574A). Use I2C scanner to find your address.
lcd.printTooltip: Print text at the current cursor position on the LCD.
lcd.printAtTooltip: Move cursor to (col, row) and print text. Col 0 = leftmost, Row 0 = top row.
lcd.clearTooltip: Clear all text on the LCD and move cursor to home position (0, 0).
lcd.backlightTooltip: Turn the LCD backlight on or off.

vl53l0x.initTooltip: Initialize VL53L0X ToF distance sensor via I2C (address 0x29). Returns true if found. Requires Adafruit VL53L0X library.
vl53l0x.distanceTooltip: Read distance in mm from VL53L0X. Returns 0 if out of range or error. Effective range: 30-1200mm.

as5600.initTooltip: Initialize AS5600 magnetic encoder via I2C (address 0x36). Returns true if magnet detected. Requires Seeed_Arduino_AS5600 library.
as5600.angleTooltip: Read absolute angle (0-360°) from AS5600. 12-bit resolution (0.088°/step).
as5600.rawTooltip: Read raw 12-bit value (0-4095) from AS5600. Full rotation = 4096 steps.

mpu6050.initTooltip: Initialize MPU6050 sensor via I2C. Requires Adafruit MPU6050 library.
mpu6050.updateTooltip: Read sensor values and update pitch/roll with complementary filter. Call in loop block.
mpu6050.accelTooltip: Get acceleration on specified axis (m/s²). Call mpu6050_update first.
mpu6050.gyroTooltip: Get angular velocity on specified axis (rad/s). Call mpu6050_update first.
mpu6050.tempTooltip: Get MPU6050 internal temperature (°C). Note: chip temperature, not ambient.
mpu6050.angleTooltip: Get pitch or roll angle (degrees) using complementary filter. Call mpu6050_update in loop.
mpu6050.calibrateTooltip: Calibrate while stationary. Offsets stored in RAM (mpu_offset_*). Save to NVS with Preferences blocks if needed.

bme280.initTooltip: Initialize BME280 via I2C. Default address 0x76, use 0x77 if SDO pin is HIGH.
bme280.readTooltip: Read temperature (°C), humidity (%), or pressure (hPa) from BME280.

bmp280.initTooltip: Initialize BMP280 via I2C. Note: BMP280 has no humidity sensor.
bmp280.readTooltip: Read temperature (°C) or pressure (hPa) from BMP280.
bmp280.altitudeTooltip: Calculate altitude (m) from current vs sea level pressure. Standard: 1013.25 hPa.

battery.voltageTooltip: Read battery voltage via ADC with resistor divider. Set ratio (e.g. 2.0 for equal resistors). Returns voltage in V.
battery.percentageTooltip: Convert battery voltage to percentage (0-100%). Set min/max for your battery type (Li-ion: 3.0-4.2V).

ble.uartSetuopTooltip: Initialize BLE and start Nordic UART Service (NUS). Use nRF Connect app to connect and communicate.
ble.uartWriteTooltip: Send a string to the connected smartphone via BLE Notify (NUS TX characteristic).
ble.uartOnReceiveTooltip: Execute handler when a BLE message is received. Use bleMessage variable to get the received text. Place in loop block.
ble.isConnectedTooltip: Returns true if a smartphone is currently connected via BLE.
ble.disconnectTooltip: Disconnect the currently connected BLE device and restart advertising.
ble.beaconTooltip: Start broadcasting as an iBeacon with the specified UUID, major and minor values. Detectable by smartphone apps.
ble.scanStartTooltip: Start scanning for nearby BLE devices for the specified duration. Use ble_on_device_found to handle results.
ble.onDeviceFoundTooltip: Execute handler when a BLE device is found during scan. Use bleFoundName, bleFoundAddress, bleFoundRssi variables.
ble.getRssiTooltip: Get the RSSI (signal strength) of the connected BLE device in dBm (negative, closer to 0 = stronger signal).
ble.initTooltip: Initialize BLE stack. Call before ble_add_service. Use ble_uart_setup instead if only using Nordic UART.
ble.addServiceTooltip: Create a custom GATT service with the given UUID. Add characteristics next, then call ble_start_advertising.
ble.addCharTooltip: Add a characteristic to a GATT service. Check read/write/notify as needed.
ble.notifyTooltip: Send a notify to the connected device via the specified characteristic UUID. Notify must be enabled on the characteristic.
ble.onWriteTooltip: Execute handler when the specified characteristic is written. Use bleMessage for the received value. Place in loop block.
ble.startAdvTooltip: Start BLE advertising after all services and characteristics are added. Smartphones can then discover and connect to this device.

ntp.syncTooltip: Synchronize time from NTP server. Timezone offset in seconds (JST=32400, UTC=0). Requires WiFi connection.
ntp.unixtimeTooltip: Get current Unix timestamp (seconds since 1970-01-01). Returns 0 if not synchronized.
ntp.formattedTooltip: Get current time as a formatted string. Format: %Y=year, %m=month, %d=day, %H=hour, %M=minute, %S=second.
ntp.componentTooltip: Get a specific component of the current time (year, month, day, hour, minute, or second).

rtc.initTooltip: Initialize RTC module (DS3231 or DS1307) via I2C. Returns true if found. Requires RTClib library.
rtc.setTimeTooltip: Set the RTC date and time.
rtc.getComponentTooltip: Get a time component from the RTC module.
rtc.formattedTooltip: Get RTC time as "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS" formatted string.

preferences.beginTooltip: Open a Preferences namespace for read/write. ESP32: NVS flash storage. RP2040: EEPROM emulation.
preferences.endTooltip: Close the Preferences namespace and free resources.
preferences.putTooltip: Save a value (int/float/string) to Preferences storage with the given key.
preferences.getTooltip: Read a value from Preferences storage. Returns the default value if the key does not exist.
preferences.removeTooltip: Delete a specific key from Preferences storage.
preferences.clearTooltip: Delete all keys in the current Preferences namespace.

eeprom.writeTooltip: Write a byte value to EEPROM at the specified address (0-511). EEPROM.commit() is called automatically.
eeprom.readTooltip: Read a byte value from EEPROM at the specified address (0-511).

sd.beginTooltip: Initialize SD card with the specified CS pin. Returns true if successful.
sd.writeTooltip: Write content to a file on SD card. Append adds to existing file, Overwrite replaces it.
sd.readTooltip: Read all contents of a file from SD card as a String.
sd.existsTooltip: Check if a file exists on SD card. Returns true if found.
sd.deleteTooltip: Delete a file from SD card.
sd.csvTooltip: Append a 3-column CSV row to a file on SD card. Ideal for data logging.

fs.mountTooltip: Mount the LittleFS filesystem. Returns true if successful. ESP32 and RP2040 both supported.
fs.writeTooltip: Write content to a file in LittleFS (internal flash storage).
fs.readTooltip: Read all contents of a file from LittleFS as a String.
fs.existsTooltip: Check if a file exists in LittleFS. Returns true if found.

ticker.attachTooltip: Execute handler code periodically at the specified interval (ESP32 only, uses Ticker.h)
ticker.checkTooltip: Check and execute the ticker handler if triggered. Place this in the loop block.

blocks.math_maptooltip: Re-maps a number from one range to another (Arduino map function)

ultrasonic.initTooltip: Initialize HC-SR04 ultrasonic sensor (HP Robot ESP32: Trig=GPIO18, Echo=GPIO19 #1)

rus04.initTooltip: Initialize RUS-04 ultrasonic sensor (RGB built-in). HC-SR04 compatible + WS2812B LED×6
rus04.rgbTooltip: Set RUS-04 eye (RGB LED) colors individually for left and right
rus04.bothSimpleTooltip: Set both eyes color via dropdown

touch.initTooltip: Initialize capacitive touch sensor (ESP32 touch pins: T0=GPIO4, T2=GPIO2, etc.)
touch.readTooltip: Returns whether the touch sensor is touched
touch.valueTooltip: Returns raw touch sensor value (smaller = stronger touch)

sound.initTooltip: Initialize analog sound sensor/microphone (ADC pins: GPIO32-39)

audio.buzzerToneTooltip: Play a tone on the buzzer

setup.label: Setup
setup.tooltip: Runs once when the program starts

loop.label: Loop
loop.tooltip: Runs repeatedly while the program is running

digitalWrite.tooltip: Write HIGH(1) or LOW(0) to digital pin

millis.label: Elapsed time (ms)
millis.tooltip: Returns elapsed time since program start in milliseconds

serialBegin.label: Serial begin
serialBegin.tooltip: Start serial communication at specified baud rate

serialPrint.label: Serial print
serialPrint.tooltip: Output value to serial monitor (no newline)

serialPrintln.label: Serial println
serialPrintln.tooltip: Output value to serial monitor with newline

servoWrite.tooltip: Move servo motor to specified angle (0-180 degrees) [Premium]

dhtRead.tooltip: Read temperature or humidity from DHT11/DHT22 sensor [Premium]

stepper.moveTooltip: Move motor by specified number of steps
stepper.stopTooltip: Stop motor and release coil current

motor.moveTooltip: Move motor in specified direction and speed (0-255)

servo.writeValueTooltip: Move servo to angle from variable
servo.sweepTooltip: Move servo from start angle to end angle

neopixel.colorSimpleTooltip: Set all LEDs to specified color

diffDrive.initTooltip: Initialize differential drive robot. Track width is distance between wheels.
diffDrive.setSpeedTooltip: Set left/right motor speeds independently (-255 to 255)
diffDrive.spinTooltip: Rotate left or right in place
diffDrive.lineTraceTooltip: Line trace driving with PID correction

pid.initTooltip: Initialize PID controller. Kp=proportional, Ki=integral, Kd=derivative gain
pid.name: Name
pid.resetTooltip: Reset PID integral and derivative values
pid.lineTracePidTooltip: Returns PID-controlled motor speed for line tracing
pid.motorSpeedCalcTooltip: Calculate left/right motor speeds with PID and store in global variables

http.getTooltip: Send a GET request to the specified URL and get the response
http.getWithHeadersTooltip: Send a GET request with custom headers (for API Key auth, etc.)

http.buildUrlTooltip: Add query parameters to a base URL

json.parseSizeTooltip: Increase buffer size for parsing large JSON

json.getNumberTooltip: Get a number value from JSON by key
json.getIntTooltip: Get an integer value from JSON by key
json.getBoolTooltip: Get a boolean value from JSON by key

json.hasKeyTooltip: Check if the key exists in JSON

json.setStringTooltip: Set a string value in JSON
json.setNumberTooltip: Set a number value in JSON
json.setBoolTooltip: Set a boolean value in JSON
json.toStringTooltip: Convert the created JSON object to a string
json.toStringPrettyTooltip: Convert the created JSON object to a formatted string

mqtt.setupTooltip: Configure WiFi and MQTT broker connection

mqtt.publishTooltip: Publish a message to the specified topic

mqtt.messageValueTooltip: Get the content of the received message
mqtt.isConnectedTooltip: Check if connected to MQTT broker
mqtt.setBufferSizeTooltip: Set the maximum MQTT message size (default 256 bytes)
mqtt.lastWillTooltip: Set the message the broker sends when client disconnects unexpectedly
mqtt.connectWithLwtTooltip: Connect to MQTT broker including Last Will settings
mqtt.publishQosTooltip: Publish a message with specified QoS level

wifi.connectTooltip: Connect to a WiFi network using SSID and password
wifi.getIpTooltip: Get the current IP address as a string
wifi.getRssiTooltip: Get WiFi signal strength in dBm (-30:very strong ~ -90:very weak)

ota.setupSimpleTooltip: Enable OTA when already connected to WiFi (use after mqtt_setup or ha_device_init)
ota.loopTooltip: Check for OTA updates. Call in loop().

esp.deepSleepTooltip: Enter deep sleep for the specified seconds (power saving mode)
esp.lightSleepTooltip: Enter light sleep for the specified seconds (resumes after)

esp.getChipIdTooltip: Get the unique ESP32 chip ID
esp.getCpuFreqTooltip: Get the CPU frequency in MHz
esp.getFlashSizeTooltip: Get the flash memory size in bytes
esp.getSketchSizeTooltip: Get the current sketch size in bytes
esp.getFreeSketchSpaceTooltip: Get the free sketch space for OTA in bytes

ha.binarySensorUpdateTooltip: Send binary sensor state to Home Assistant
ha.switchCreateTooltip: Create a switch controllable from Home Assistant

ha.switchSetStateTooltip: Send switch state to Home Assistant
ha.lightCreateTooltip: Create a light controllable from Home Assistant
ha.lightOnCommandTooltip: Execute when light command is received. ha_light_state and ha_light_brightness are available.
ha.lightStateTooltip: Get the received light ON/OFF state
ha.lightSetStateTooltip: Report light state to Home Assistant
ha.buttonCreateTooltip: Create a button that can be pressed from Home Assistant
ha.buttonOnPressTooltip: Execute when button is pressed from Home Assistant
ha.loopTooltip: Maintain communication with Home Assistant. Call in loop().
ha.isConnectedTooltip: Check if connected to Home Assistant
ha.numberCreateTooltip: Create an entity that accepts numeric input from Home Assistant
ha.numberOnCommandTooltip: Execute when number is changed from Home Assistant. ha_number_value is available.
ha.numberSetStateTooltip: Report number state to Home Assistant
ha.fanCreateTooltip: Create a fan controllable from Home Assistant

ha.fanStateTooltip: Get the received fan ON/OFF state
ha.fanSetStateTooltip: Report fan state to Home Assistant
ha.coverCreateTooltip: Create a cover (shutter, etc.) controllable from Home Assistant
ha.coverOnCommandTooltip: Execute when cover command is received from Home Assistant
ha.coverSetStateTooltip: Report cover state to Home Assistant

ha.lightRgbBTooltip: Get the received RGB blue component (0-255)

firmware.description: Write Arduino C++ firmware to ESP32 (WiFi OTA or BLE supported)
firmware.step1Desc: Connect ESP32 to your PC via USB cable
firmware.step3Desc: Click the button to show device selection dialog. Select ESP32 to start writing.
firmware.webSerialNotSupported: Web Serial API is not supported.
firmware.useChromeOrEdge: Please use Chrome or Edge browser.
firmware.afterInstall: After firmware installation, you can start visual programming with Blockly in the editor page. Generated Arduino C++ code can be updated via OTA (wireless).
firmware.usbDriverDesc: If device is not recognized, install USB serial driver:
firmware.usbDriverNote: Drivers for CP2102, CH340, FTDI, and other chips are required.
firmware.title: Firmware Installer
firmware.bleFirmwareDesc: Bluetooth upload supported, required for initial upload
firmware.eraseFlashDesc: Erasing the entire flash will delete all saved data (UUID, WiFi settings, etc.) and reset to factory state.
firmware.connectUsbFirst: ※ Please connect ESP32 via USB first
firmware.eraseConfirm: This will erase the entire flash. All saved data (UUID, WiFi settings, etc.) will be deleted. Are you sure?
firmware.installDesc: Press INSTALL to connect ESP32 via USB and write {{type}} firmware{{version}}.
firmware.autoConnectFailed: 💡 Auto-connect failed. Please enter BOOT mode manually and retry.
firmware.eraseCompleteReset: ✅ Flash erase complete. Please reset ESP32.
firmware.connectUsbInstruction: Connect ESP32 to PC with USB cable
firmware.selectPortInstruction: After connecting, press the button below to select the port
firmware.followStepsInstruction: Follow these steps to put ESP32 in flash mode, then press retry:
firmware.bootStep2: While holding BOOT, press and release the EN button
firmware.bootHint: Hint: Some boards label the BOOT button as IO0, FLASH, or GPIO0
firmware.installDescription: Press INSTALL button to connect ESP32 via USB and flash {{firmwareType}} firmware{{version}}.
firmware.eraseFlashDescription: Erasing the entire flash will delete all stored data (UUID, WiFi settings, etc.) and reset to initial state.
firmware.connectFirstNote: * Please Connect ESP32 via USB first
firmware.bleInstallerDescription: Write the ESP32 BLE OTA firmware via USB. Required for the first flash.
firmware.wifiInstallerDescription: Write the ESP32 WiFi OTA firmware via USB. Required for the first flash.

status.webSerialUnsupported: Web Serial API is not supported by this browser. Use Chrome or Edge.
status.webSerialUnsupportedShort: Web Serial API is not supported by this browser
status.webBluetoothUnsupported: Web Bluetooth API is not supported by this browser. Use Chrome or Edge.
status.otaSuccess: ✓ OTA update complete! Device rebooted successfully.
status.otaNoResponse: ⚠️ Upload complete, but the device is not responding. Please check the device power and WiFi connection.
status.writeSuccessMicropython: ✅ Firmware write complete! ESP32 will boot with MicroPython.
status.cannotConnect:
Cannot connect to device: {{url}}
If the device just rebooted, please wait ~10 seconds and retry.
status.otaTimeout: OTA update timeout: did not complete within {{sec}} seconds. Please check the device power.

duplicate.title: Duplicate this class
duplicate.description: Creates a new class by copying assignment templates and attached PDFs. Students and submissions are not duplicated.

export.title: Save class records
export.description: This ZIP is a snapshot of grades and submissions. It includes assignment templates, attached PDFs, student list, submissions, grading results, and comments.
export.noReimport: * This file cannot be re-imported into DigiCode to restore the class. To start a new class with the same materials, use "Duplicate this class".

deleteClass.confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this class?
deleteClass.warning: All student accounts belonging to this class will also be deleted. This action cannot be undone.
deleteClass.hint: If needed, use "Save class records" to keep a copy of grades before deleting.
